title: Multiple-Step Income Statement
event: Ortiz Co. reports net sales of $800,000, cost of goods sold of $480,000, selling expenses of $90,000, administrative expenses of $70,000, interest expense of $12,000, and an $8,000 gain on sale of equipment. The tax rate is 25%. Compute gross profit, operating income, and net income.

computationPaths.narration: The multiple-step format tells a story in tiers: trading margin, then the cost of running the operation, then items outside the core business, then tax.

memorize.body: Sales − COGS = Gross profit → − operating expenses = Operating income → ± other items = Pretax → − tax = Net income.
Interest and gains/losses sit BELOW operating income — they describe financing and disposals, not operations.

questions.prompt: Using the facts above, OPERATING income = ?

distractors.feedback: You pulled the interest and gain into operations. Those are non-operating — operating income stops at 160,000.
That's gross profit. Operating income also absorbs selling and administrative expenses.
